content_markdown:
P and S are two points on a gravitational equipotential surface around a planet. Q and R are two points on a different gravitational equipotential surface at a greater distance from the planet.

The greatest work done by the gravitational force is when moving a mass from

diagram_metadata.description: A central planet surrounded by two concentric dashed circles representing equipotential surfaces. Points P and S are on the inner circle. Points Q and R are on the outer circle.

vibe_explanation: Work done by the gravitational force is given by $W = -m\Delta V$. Moving along an equipotential surface (P to S or Q to R) results in zero work because $\Delta V = 0$. Moving from a higher potential (further away, R) to a lower potential (closer, P) means the gravitational force is acting in the direction of motion, doing positive work. Moving from S to R (lower to higher potential) requires work to be done against the gravitational force, meaning the force itself does negative work.
